title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Inspector Corradino questions Mona Harris regarding Rudi Scherz. While she describes him as a gentleman, she suggests he may have been a fraud. She notes that although Scherz claimed to be from a wealthy Swiss family, his clothing was inexpensive and his stories about climbing the Alps contradicted his tendency to feel dizzy on simple ridges. She is uncertain if he mentioned Chipping Clegg-horn, though she recalls him inquiring about bus schedules.

Corradino then travels to a neglected estate to meet Chief Constable Fletcher. Fletcher reports that there were no signs of forced entry and that Scherz likely arrived by bus at six o'clock. A discrepancy exists regarding the house's security: Miss Blacklock believes the front door is usually only locked at night, but the maid claims it was locked all afternoon. Fletcher also notes that the lighting system had been manipulated, though it is unclear how the fuse box in the pantry was tampered with without the maid noticing.

Upon entering the house, Corradino is informed by a young woman that Mickey has locked herself in her room and is making moaning sounds. Corradino meets Miss Blacklock, an elegant woman wearing a bandage on her left ear, and her anxious companion, Dora Bunner. While surveying the Victorian living room, Corradino notices a silver vase with wilted violets, which he finds out of place in the well-maintained home. Miss Bunner describes the chaos of the previous night, including broken furniture and a cigarette burn. Miss Blacklock explains that she met Scherz three weeks prior at the Medenham Pleasure Gardens, where he claimed to be the son of the owner of the Alpine Hotel in Monte Carlo.
